issues_found.description: WIN-070 in our review has no corresponding WIN-070 on the dome site (dome ends at WIN-069). Our WIN-070 claim ('Forensic Zoom chart proves κ=1.67 nT/µGal via L1A induction-residual analysis') may be based on content the dome has removed or renumbered.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Verify WIN-070 source on dome site. Confirm whether the dome added WIN-070 or whether our review covers a claim that existed in an earlier version.

issues_found.description: Dome site has review-response.html (title: 'Review Response & Strawman Control') still reachable at john09289.github.io/predictions/review-response.html. ISS-2206 remains open for analyst assessment.

summary: Overall status: PASS. Test suite: 3692/3692 (0 failed). Build reproducibility: PASS — git HEAD is self-consistent; a fresh build from the current wins.json matches git HEAD docs/index.html exactly. The only diff seen was between FUSE workspace (stale) and git, which is expected FUSE staleness that workspace-sync will resolve. All internal anchors, tab structure, discoverability infrastructure, and progressive disclosure checks pass. External links: 11/12 key data sources return 200; podaac-tools.jpl.nasa.gov continues to time out (ISS-2204, minor, tracked). Expansion tracker is healthy: next_id=435 > max_id=434, live/archive disjoint, no orphans, no stale pending. 1 unprocessed curmudgeon review (WIN-063.c8.json, reviewed yesterday) — normal pipeline lag. Drift audit is 67.6h old (within 72h threshold). 4 open issues tracked from prior runs: ISS-1877 (major, EXP-434 needs integration), ISS-2204 (minor, podaac timeout), ISS-2205 (minor, WIN-070 dome alignment), ISS-2206 (informational, dome review-response.html). No new untracked issues found this run.
